evals.prompt: Where does /debug file the GH issue?
evals.expected_output: sonthanh/ai-brain — the central tracker per skill-process-pocock-adoption decision
expectations: Does NOT file in the affected source repo

evals.prompt: Should /debug fix the bug it finds?
evals.expected_output: No — /debug only diagnoses and files the issue. The fix happens via /impl + /tdd in a fresh context.
expectations: Mentions /tdd or /impl picks it up later
Mentions fresh context for the fix

evals.prompt: What investigation surface must the explore subagent cover?
evals.expected_output: Source repo, config, cloud RemoteTrigger, local launchd, vault state, recent commits, and MCP availability — not just code
expectations: Mentions vault state or outcome logs

evals.prompt: Why must /debug spawn a subagent rather than investigate in the parent context?
evals.expected_output: Investigation reads many files; parent context would blow past the smart zone and degrade the fix-plan synthesis. The Explore subagent returns a tight summary.
expectations: Mentions smart zone or context size

evals.prompt: Should the issue body include file paths and line numbers?
evals.expected_output: No — paths and line numbers rot on refactor. Issue body describes behaviors and contracts, not implementation locations.
expectations: Mentions describe behavior or describe contract

evals.prompt: What is the structure of the /tdd Fix Plan inside the issue body?
evals.expected_output: Numbered list of vertical-slice tracer-bullet RED-GREEN cycles, one test → one impl per cycle, optional final REFACTOR step
expectations: Mentions vertical slice or tracer bullet

evals.prompt: What does /debug do when the explore comes back ambiguous?
evals.expected_output: Do NOT file the issue — re-spawn Explore with a tighter brief, or ask the user one targeted question. Filing vague issues makes /tdd flail.

evals.prompt: What labels should /debug apply to the GH issue?
How many clarifying questions can /debug ask before investigating?
evals.expected_output: At most one — start investigating after the first answer. Two questions is one too many.
expectations: Mentions at most one or one question

evals.expected_output: One-line typos in vault docs, brand-new skills with no baseline, when user already has root cause + plan, regressions to code written in this session
expectations: Mentions brand new skill or no baseline
